meta.title: Dropped Objects Prevention — Platform Bravo drilling derrick
meta.subtitle: DROPS survey & register, monkeyboard to drill floor (worked example)
meta.author: HSE Marine & Offshore
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This DROPS survey covers the Platform Bravo drilling derrick (monkeyboard to drill floor, UKCS). The control posture is reliable securing first: every at-height item is surveyed and secured with a primary fixing plus secondary retention, with an exclusion zone below at-height work. Consequence bands trace to the public m·g·h method using the user-supplied mass and fall height; the user's licensed DROPS Calculator bands are recorded and the licensed threshold values are not recomputed.
Lead control: reliable securing (primary fixing + secondary retention) + DROPS survey + exclusion zone below the derrick — 'hard hats below' is the residual, not the headline.

items: Installation/area: Platform Bravo drilling derrick, monkeyboard to drill floor (UKCS)
At-height structures surveyed: derrick, monkeyboard & fingerboard, racking-board lighting
Fixed and potential dropped objects identified per DROPS Recommended Practice (2017)

rows: Major (user DROPS Calculator band); licensed thresholds [GAP]
Primary fixing only — secondary retention [GAP] (finding F-01)
[GAP] — user has not confirmed a DROPS band
[GAP] — band cannot be set without mass
Operational control + exclusion zone below the derrick
sections.caption: Dropped-object register — taxonomy, public m·g·h impact energy (user-supplied mass/height), user's DROPS band ([GAP] where unconfirmed; licensed thresholds never reproduced), securing status

rows.title: Fingerboard latch pin has no secondary retention
rows.description: F-01: the monkeyboard fingerboard latch pin (4 kg, 28 m, ≈ 1098 J) has a primary fixing only — no recorded secondary retention. Flagged as an immediate high-priority finding pending the reliable-securing standard.

rows.title: Swung-tubular mass not supplied
rows.description: F-02: the dynamic swung-tubular object has no supplied mass, so its impact energy and consequence band stay [GAP] — never invented. The mass must be confirmed to band the consequence.

rows.control: Remove non-essential items from the monkeyboard; design out the dropped-object source
Fit secondary retention (tethered lanyard) to the fingerboard latch pin to the reliable-securing standard
DROPS survey + register, weekly at-height inspection, exclusion/red zone below the derrick during tripping
Hard hats for persons who must enter the drill floor — residual only, supplementing securing + exclusion

rows.action: Fit and record secondary retention on the fingerboard latch pin (close F-01)
Obtain the swung-tubular mass and band its consequence via the public m·g·h method (close F-02 [GAP])
Confirm the licensed DROPS Calculator consequence bands with the SME and close the racking-light band [GAP]

sections.text: [ASSUMPTION A1] The licensed DROPS Calculator consequence-band threshold values stay [GAP] / user-confirmed; the user's bands are recorded as supplied and never recomputed from the licensed table.

items: DROPS Recommended Practice (2017) + DROPS Reliable Securing — survey, static/dynamic taxonomy, reliable-securing hierarchy, exclusion zones
IADC HSE Guidelines Section 16 (Dropped Objects); API RP 2D (offshore crane O&M) / API RP 54 (drilling & servicing)
